note: Replay plan events were derived from effective adapter replay_events generated from source test evidence.

events.description: Replay of cJSON test: add empty array to object, retrieve and verify type
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c line 466: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL and TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(array->type, cJSON_Array)

event_metadata.normalization: Exact variant equality via pattern matching

input.case: Create object, call cJSON_AddArrayToObject with key "array", then cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive and check type

expected.observable_behavior: Retrieved element is non-null and its type equals Array

events.description: Replay of cJSON test: add true and false booleans to object, retrieve and verify types
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c line 446: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL and TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT on both true and false items

event_metadata.normalization: Exact variant equality via pattern matching

input.case: Create object, call cJSON_AddBoolToObject for true and false, then retrieve via cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive and check types

expected.observable_behavior: Retrieved items are non-null and have types Boolean(true) and Boolean(false) respectively

events.description: Replay of cJSON test: add false boolean to object, retrieve and verify type
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c line 442: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL and TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(false_item->type, cJSON_False)

event_metadata.normalization: Exact variant equality via pattern matching

input.case: Create object, call cJSON_AddFalseToObject with key "false", then retrieve via cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive and check type

expected.observable_behavior: Retrieved item is non-null and has type Boolean(false)

events.description: Replay of cJSON test: add null value to object, retrieve and verify type
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c line 429: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL and TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(null->type, cJSON_NULL)

event_metadata.normalization: Exact variant equality via pattern matching

input.case: Create object, call cJSON_AddNullToObject with key "null", then retrieve via cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive and check type

expected.observable_behavior: Retrieved item is non-null and has type Null

events.description: Add a number to object and verify type and value
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c::cjson_add_number_should_add_number (assertions: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL, TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT, TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_DOUBLE)
events.input: Create object, add number key 'number' with value 42, retrieve and verify.
events.expected: Non-null item with type cJSON_Number, valuedouble 42, valueint 42

events.description: Add an empty object to object and verify type
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c::cJSON_add_object_should_add_object (assertions: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL, TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT)
events.input: Create root, add object key 'object' with empty object, retrieve and verify.

events.description: Add a raw JSON string to object and verify type and value
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c::cjson_add_raw_should_add_raw (assertions: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL, TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT, TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_STRING)
events.input: Create root, add raw key 'raw' with value '{}', retrieve and verify.
events.expected: Non-null item with type cJSON_Raw and valuestring '{}'

events.description: Create object, add a string property, retrieve it, and verify type and value.
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c:454-459 (cjson_add_string_should_add_string)

input.case: Create object, add string 'Hello World!' under key 'string', then retrieve and assert

expected.observable_behavior: After adding a string property, the object item at that key is a string with the correct value.

events.description: Create object, add a boolean true property, retrieve it, and verify type.
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c:433-441 (cjson_add_true_should_add_true)

input.case: Create object, add true under key 'true', then retrieve and assert type is Boolean.

expected.observable_behavior: After adding a boolean true, the object item at that key is a boolean with value true.

events.description: Parse two JSON arrays and verify they are equal with case-sensitive and case-insensitive comparison.
events.evidence: tests/compare_tests.c:204-231 (cjson_compare_should_compare_arrays)

input.case: Compare empty arrays [] with [] and complex arrays with mixed types.

expected.observable_behavior: Parsing equal JSON arrays yields values that compare equal (both case-sensitive and case-insensitive).

events.description: Parse boolean values and verify they compare equal.
events.evidence: tests/compare_tests.c:199-213 (cjson_compare_should_compare_booleans)

input.case: Compare "true" with "true" and "false" with "false", both case-sensitive and case-insensitive.

expected.observable_behavior: Parsing equal boolean JSON values yields values that compare equal.

events.description: Create a string array from a list of strings under allocation failure condition, expecting null.
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c line 440: TEST_ASSERT_NULL(cJSON_CreateStringArray(strings, 3))

input.case: const char* strings[] = {"1", "2", "3"}; count=3

expected.observable_behavior: JsonValue::Null (equivalent to C NULL pointer)

events.description: Compare null JSON values
events.evidence: Source file tests/compare_tests.c, assertions TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(compare_from_string("null","null",true)) and TEST_ASSERT_FALSE(compare_from_string("null","true",true))

input.case: parse "null" and "null", then compare; also parse "null" and "true", then compare

expected.observable_behavior: true for equal nulls, false for null vs true

events.description: Compare number JSON values
events.evidence: Source file tests/compare_tests.c, assertions TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(compare_from_string("1","1",true)), TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(compare_from_string("0.0001","0.0001",true))

input.case: parse number strings and compare

expected.observable_behavior: equal numbers compare as true, unequal as false

events.description: Compare object JSON values
events.evidence: Source file tests/compare_tests.c, assertions TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(compare_from_string("{}","{}",true)), TEST_ASSERT_FALSE(compare_from_string(...))

input.case: parse object strings and compare

expected.observable_behavior: equal objects compare as true, unequal as false

events.description: Compare string JSON values
events.evidence: Source file tests/compare_tests.c, assertions TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(compare_from_string("\"abcdefg\"","\"abcdefg\"",true)), TEST_ASSERT_FALSE(compare_from_string("\"ABCDEFG\"","\"abcdefg\"",true))

input.case: parse string literals and compare

expected.observable_behavior: equal strings compare as true, case-sensitive comparison (source uses case_sensitive=true) returns false for different case

events.description: Add item to object with aliased string (no use-after-free in Rust)
events.evidence: Source file tests/misc_tests.c, test that creating an object, a number with aliased string, and inserting does not cause use-after-free. In Rust, ownership prevents aliasing issues.

event_metadata.normalization: observable: insert succeeds without memory errors

input.case: create object, create number, insert with key 'number'

expected.observable_behavior: insert returns Ok(())

events.description: cJSON_GetStringValue should return the string value for a string JsonValue, and NULL for number and null
events.evidence: tests/misc_tests.c line 825: TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(cJSON_GetStringValue(string) == string->valuestring); TEST_ASSERT_NULL(cJSON_GetStringValue(number)); TEST_ASSERT_NULL(cJSON_GetStringValue(NULL));

event_metadata.normalization: as_str returns Option<&str>: Some corresponds to string equality, None corresponds to NULL

input.case: string='test', number=1, null value

expected.observable_behavior: JsonValue::from('test').as_str() should return Some('test'); JsonValue::from(1).as_str() should return None; JsonValue::Null.as_str() should return None

events.description: Parse an object, add an array 'rd' with two items, delete first item, and verify output strings

input.case: Parse '{}', create array, add two JSON objects, delete index 0

expected.observable_behavior: After each step, dump of root matches the expected JSON strings: first with one item, then with two items, then with second item remaining

events.description: Parse valid and invalid big number JSON strings

events.description: Replace item in object preserves key name
events.evidence: misc_tests.c line 819: TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(root->child == replacement) and TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_STRING("child", replacement->string)

expected.observable_behavior: After replacement, the object's key 'child' maps to the new value (2) and the key name is preserved.

events.description: Replace items in array via indexing

input.case: array with three null items replaced by numbers 0,1,2

expected.observable_behavior: After replacement, array contains the new values at corresponding indices.
